item.title: 如何在本地主机 (https://localhost/) 上设置 SSL？ | How can I set up SSL on localhost (https://localhost/)?
item.description:
艾米丽·弗利亚斯

发表于 4 月 26 日

在我们开始之前，重要的是要注意 SSL 证书通常是为域名颁发的，而不是 IP 地址或本地主机。 但是，您仍然可以为本地主机创建可用于测试目的的自签名 SSL 证书。

以下是在本地主机上设置 SSL 的步骤：

生成自签名 SSL 证书

在 Windows 上，您可以使用 OpenSSL 或 XAMPP 等工具生成自签名 SSL 证书。 在 Linux 或 macOS 上，您可以使用 OpenSSL 命令行工具。 打开命令提示符或终端并导航到要生成 SSL 证书的目录。 输入以下命令生成私钥：

 openssl genrsa -out key.pem 2048 openssl req -new -key key.pem -out csr.pem

输入以下命令以生成自签名 SSL 证书：

 openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in csr.pem -signkey key.pem -out cert.pem

这将生成一个自签名 SSL 证书并将其保存到当前目录中名为“cert.pem”的文件中。
安装 SSL 证书

在 Windows 上，您可以通过双击 cert.pem 文件并按照证书导入向导中的提示来安装 SSL 证书。 在 Linux 或 macOS 上，您可以将 cert.pem 文件复制到 /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ 目录，然后运行以下命令来更新系统的证书存储：

 sudo update-ca-certificates

配置您的 Web 服务器以使用 SSL

如果您使用的是 Apache，则可以通过取消注释httpd.conf文件中的以下行来启用 SSL：

 LoadModule ssl_module modules/mod_ssl.so

然后，为您的本地主机站点创建一个使用 SSL 证书的虚拟主机：

 <VirtualHost *:443> ServerName localhost DocumentRoot /path/to/localhost SSLEngine on SSLCertificateFile /path/to/cert.pem SSLCertificateKeyFile /path/to/key.pem </VirtualHost>

将 /path/to/localhost 替换为您的本地主机站点目录的路径，并将 /path/to/cert.pem 和 /path/to/key.pem 分别替换为您的 SSL 证书和私钥文件的路径。 如果您使用的是 Nginx，则可以通过将以下行添加到您的服务器块来启用 SSL：

 listen 443 ssl; ssl_certificate /path/to/cert.pem; ssl_certificate_key /path/to/key.pem;

同样，将 /path/to/cert.pem 和 /path/to/key.pem 分别替换为您的 SSL 证书和私钥文件的路径。
测试 SSL 连接

打开 Web 浏览器并导航到localhost/和http://localhost/ 。 您应该会看到一条警告，指出该站点的证书不受信任，因为它是自签名的。 但是，您应该仍然可以继续访问该站点。
如果您使用的是 Chrome，您可能需要点击地址栏中的“不安全”按钮，然后点击“证书（无效）”才能查看证书。

如何使用 SSL 在本地主机上运行 phpmyadmin？

  • 按照我上面回答的步骤生成自签名 SSL 证书并配置 Apache 以使用 SSL。
  • 打开 phpMyAdmin 配置文件，它位于 XAMPP 安装的“phpmyadmin”目录中（例如，C:\xampp\phpmyadmin\config.inc.php）。
  • 将以下行添加到文件末尾：
 $cfg['ForceSSL'] = true; $cfg['SSLVerify'] = false;
  • 第一行强制 phpMyAdmin 使用 SSL，第二行禁用 SSL 验证。 由于您使用的是自签名证书，除非您将证书作为受信任的证书安装在您的计算机上，否则 SSL 验证将失败，但出于开发目的，可以禁用它。
  • 保存对配置文件的更改。
  • 在 XAMPP 控制面板中重新启动 Apache。
  • 打开 Web 浏览器并导航到https://localhost/phpmyadmin 。 您现在应该能够使用 SSL 访问 phpMyAdmin。 您可能会看到有关自签名证书的警告，但您应该能够继续访问该站点。

一旦暂停，emily-flias 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，emily-flias 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，emily-flias 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 emily-flias 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 emily-flias 的方法：

降低 emily-flias 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 emily-flias 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Emily Flias

Posted on Apr 26

Before we start, it's important to note that SSL certificates are typically issued for domain names, not IP addresses or localhost. However, you can still create a self-signed SSL certificate for localhost that can be used for testing purposes.

Here are the steps to set up SSL on localhost:

Generate a self-signed SSL certificate

On Windows, you can use a tool like OpenSSL or XAMPP to generate a self-signed SSL certificate. On Linux or macOS, you can use the OpenSSL command-line tool. Open a command prompt or terminal and navigate to the directory where you want to generate the SSL certificate. Enter the following command to generate a private key:

openssl genrsa -out key.pem 2048
openssl req -new -key key.pem -out csr.pem

Enter the following command to generate a self-signed SSL certificate:

openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in csr.pem -signkey key.pem -out cert.pem

This will generate a self-signed SSL certificate and save it to a file called "cert.pem" in the current directory.
Install the SSL certificate

On Windows, you can install the SSL certificate by double-clicking the cert.pem file and following the prompts in the Certificate Import Wizard. On Linux or macOS, you can copy the cert.pem file to the /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ directory and then run the following command to update the system's certificate store:

sudo update-ca-certificates

Configure your web server to use SSL

If you're using Apache, you can enable SSL by uncommenting the following line in the httpd.conf file:

LoadModule ssl_module modules/mod_ssl.so

Then, create a virtual host for your localhost site that uses the SSL certificate:

<VirtualHost *:443>
    ServerName localhost
    DocumentRoot /path/to/localhost
    SSLEngine on
    SSLCertificateFile /path/to/cert.pem
    SSLCertificateKeyFile /path/to/key.pem
</VirtualHost>

Replace /path/to/localhost with the path to your localhost site's directory, and replace /path/to/cert.pem and /path/to/key.pem with the paths to your SSL certificate and private key files, respectively. If you're using Nginx, you can enable SSL by adding the following lines to your server block:

listen 443 ssl;
ssl_certificate /path/to/cert.pem;
ssl_certificate_key /path/to/key.pem;

Again, replace /path/to/cert.pem and /path/to/key.pem with the paths to your SSL certificate and private key files, respectively.
Test the SSL connection

Open a web browser and navigate to localhost/ and http://localhost/. You should see a warning that the site's certificate is not trusted, since it's self-signed. However, you should still be able to proceed to the site.
If you're using Chrome, you may need to click the "Not Secure" button in the address bar and then click "Certificate (invalid)" to view the certificate.

How can I run phpmyadmin on localhost with SSL?

  • Follow the steps to generate a self-signed SSL certificate and configure Apache to use SSL as i said above answer.
  • Open the phpMyAdmin configuration file, which is located in the "phpmyadmin" directory of your XAMPP installation (e.g., C:\xampp\phpmyadmin\config.inc.php).
  • Add the following lines to the end of the file: 
$cfg['ForceSSL'] = true;
$cfg['SSLVerify'] = false;
  • The first line forces phpMyAdmin to use SSL, and the second line disables SSL verification. Since you're using a self-signed certificate, the SSL verification will fail unless you install the certificate as a trusted certificate on your computer, but for development purposes, it's okay to disable it.
  • Save the changes to the configuration file.
  • Restart Apache in the XAMPP control panel.
  • Open a web browser and navigate to https://localhost/phpmyadmin. You should now be able to access phpMyAdmin using SSL. You may see a warning about the self-signed certificate, but you should be able to proceed to the site.

CodewithGuillaume - Mar 26

Once suspended, emily-flias will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, emily-flias will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by emily-flias will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If emily-flias is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag emily-flias:

Make all posts by emily-flias less visible

emily-flias consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging emily-flias will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: emily-flias
